response: This code implements the naive triple-nested loop algorithm for standard matrix multiplication: it takes two compatible matrices A and B (where the number of columns of A matches the number of rows of B), initializes an m×p result matrix (with m as the row count of A and p as the column count of B), then fills each element `result[i][j]` as the dot product of the i-th row of A and j-th column of B by summing `A[i][k] * B[k][j]` across the shared inner dimension k.
